criteria.criterion: Criterion A: ≥5 of 9 symptoms during the same 2-week period, representing a change from prior functioning. At least one symptom must be (1) depressed mood or (2) loss of interest or pleasure (anhedonia). Symptoms: 1. Depressed mood most of the day... 2. Markedly diminished interest or pleasure... 3. Significant weight/appetite change... 4. Insomnia or hypersomnia... 5. Psychomotor agitation/retardation... 6. Fatigue... 7. Worthlessness/guilt... 8. Diminished concentration... 9. Thoughts of death/suicidality.
criteria.evidence: Patient reported 'low mood 3 weeks', 'poor sleep', 'loss of interest'. Duration meets 2 weeks but only 3 symptoms reported so far; needs confirmation of total ≥5 symptoms and full 2-week period details.

criteria.criterion: Criterion B: The symptoms cause clinically significant distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning.
criteria.evidence: No report of distress or impairment provided yet.

criteria.criterion: Criterion C: The episode is not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance or another medical condition.
criteria.evidence: No information on substances or medical conditions; requires clinical judgment.

criteria.criterion: Criterion D: The episode is not better explained by schizoaffective disorder, schizophrenia, schizophreniform disorder, delusional disorder, or other specified/unspecified schizophrenia spectrum and psychotic disorders.
criteria.evidence: No information on psychotic disorders; requires clinical judgment.

criteria.criterion: Criterion E: There has never been a manic or hypomanic episode (unless these were substance-induced or attributable to a medical condition).
criteria.evidence: No history of mania/hypomania reported yet; needs screening over time.

criteria.criterion: Criterion A (Disorder): Criteria have been met for at least one hypomanic episode and at least one major depressive episode.
criteria.evidence: Major depressive episode symptoms partially reported ('low mood 3 weeks', 'poor sleep', 'loss of interest'); no hypomanic episode symptoms or history reported.

criteria.criterion: Criterion B: There has never been a manic episode.
criteria.evidence: No history of manic episode reported yet.

criteria.criterion: Criterion C: At least one hypomanic episode and at least one major depressive episode are not better explained by schizoaffective disorder and are not superimposed on schizophrenia, schizophreniform disorder, delusional disorder, or other specified/unspecified schizophrenia spectrum and psychotic disorders.
criteria.evidence: No information on psychotic disorders; requires clinical judgment.

criteria.criterion: Criterion D: The symptoms of depression OR the unpredictability caused by frequent alternation between periods of depression and hypomania cause clinically significant distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning.
criteria.evidence: No report of distress, impairment, or hypomania yet.

criteria.criterion: Hypomanic Episode Criterion A: A distinct period of abnormally and persistently elevated, expansive, or irritable mood AND abnormally and persistently increased activity or energy, lasting at least 4 consecutive days, present most of the day, nearly every day.
criteria.evidence: No report of elevated/expansive/irritable mood or increased activity/energy lasting 4 days.

criteria.criterion: Hypomanic Episode Criterion B: ≥3 symptoms (≥4 if mood only irritable) from the 7-symptom list... persisting, representing a noticeable change...
Hypomanic Episode Criterion C: The episode is associated with an unequivocal change in functioning that is uncharacteristic of the individual when not symptomatic.
Hypomanic Episode Criterion D: The disturbance in mood and the change in functioning are observable by others.
Hypomanic Episode Criterion E: The episode is NOT severe enough to cause marked impairment... or hospitalization. If psychotic features, it is manic.
Hypomanic Episode Criterion F: The episode is not attributable to the physiological effects of a substance.
criteria.evidence: No information on substances; requires clinical judgment.
